methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Ascend Elements
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Growth), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
MineSense Technologies is a Growth-stage climate and energy company that has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), though the narrowly-screened peer set proved insufficient for a robust median-based approach.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, anchors valuation to the $100M total-raised proxy and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, yielding $120M. This reflects the company's progression through its funding trajectory. A peer-median approach was not applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Climate & Energy sector—while 21 same-sector companies exist in the broader dataset, only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer met filters, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum required for statistical reliability. Inclusion of broader-sector peers risked conflating business models and capital requirements.

The triangulated analysis, based on the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $102M (low) to $138M (median $120M). This narrow analytical foundation reflects data constraints rather than precision. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer multiples to validate the 1.2x multiplier against comparable stage transitions, and limited visibility into MineSense's specific revenue, burn rate, and competitive positioning relative to peers like Redwood Materials and Helion. A refreshed valuation incorporating recent financial metrics or additional comparables would strengthen conviction in this range.
